description:
Implement CMS-Based UI Translation System for Webpage Sections

Fix the non-functional CMS editing functionality by migrating UI translation keys to use CMS schemas instead of direct TOML file editing for both Polish (pl) and English (en) languages.

Current Problem:
- CMS edition is not working for all webpage elements
- Users cannot edit sections of webpages through the CMS interface
- Translation keys are currently using direct TOML editing instead of CMS schemas

Requirements:
- Audit all webpage sections to identify which elements are not editable through CMS
- Migrate UI translation keys (pl and en) from direct TOML file editing to CMS schema-based approach
- Ensure CMS schemas are properly defined for all translatable webpage sections
- Connect frontend translation rendering to pull from CMS schemas instead of static TOML files
- Verify all webpage sections are editable through the CMS interface after migration
- Test editing functionality for both Polish and English translations
- Ensure changes made in CMS are immediately reflected on the website
- Document the new CMS schema structure for future content editors

Edge Cases to Consider:
- Handling fallback translations when CMS values are missing
- Migration of existing TOML translations to CMS without data loss
- Maintaining translation key consistency across both languages
- Proper error handling when CMS schema fails to load

Success Criteria:
- All webpage sections are editable through CMS interface
- No direct TOML file editing required for content updates
- Both pl and en translations work correctly through CMS
- Content editors can successfully modify all webpage sections
title: Migrate UI translations from TOML to CMS schemas
